picks.rationale: The Marlins are currently hot, winning 8 of their last 10 games, including two consecutive shutouts against Arizona. Despite the public heavily backing the Pirates, the moneyline for Miami has shortened, which is a strong indicator of sharp money coming in on the underdog. Pittsburgh's starter, Braxton Ashcraft, is coming off a poor outing, while Miami's Sandy Alcantara is a proven veteran. This combination of recent form, line movement, and pitching matchup creates a valuable opportunity for the Marlins.

The Detroit Tigers are on a remarkable hot streak in June, boasting a 7-2 record this month and winning three consecutive series. Their offense has seen a significant improvement, with their team OPS rising to .926 in June, ranking second in MLB, a stark contrast to their .596 OPS in May. This offensive resurgence, coupled with their strong recent performance, provides a solid foundation for a confident pick on the Tigers to continue their winning ways.

The Diamondbacks have a clear pitching advantage with Eduardo Rodriguez (5-2, 2.52 ERA) on the mound, whose team has a strong 9-4 record against the spread in his starts. In contrast, the Reds' starter, Nick Lodolo, carries a much higher 5.51 ERA. Despite both teams' recent struggles, the significant disparity in starting pitching quality makes the Diamondbacks a confident moneyline pick. The line movement also shows the Diamondbacks' moneyline shortening, indicating some sharp interest.

picks.game: Atlanta Braves vs New York Mets
picks.rationale: The Atlanta Braves are currently in a slump, holding a 3-7 record over their last 10 games and coming off two consecutive shutouts. Compounding their struggles, star player Ronald Acuña Jr. is on the 10-day injured list with a hamstring strain. The Mets' starter, Nolan McLean, has impressive underlying metrics with a 1.11 WHIP and a high strikeout rate, suggesting he is due for better results. Playing at home and facing a struggling, injured Braves team, the Mets present a strong value pick.

The Athletics are playing at a neutral site in Las Vegas against the struggling Colorado Rockies, who have a poor 26-43 record and are missing key players Kris Bryant and Zac Veen due to injury. The Athletics have demonstrated good recent form, covering the spread in four of their last five games. Furthermore, the Rockies have been noted as "horrible versus lefties," and the Athletics' bullpen provides a "considerable edge," which should enable them to secure a victory by more than 1.5 runs.

Canada's star player and captain, Alphonso Davies, is officially ruled out for this opening World Cup match due to a hamstring injury, significantly diminishing their primary attacking threat. Bosnia-Herzegovina enters this fixture on an impressive eight-match unbeaten streak, with their last five international matches all resulting in draws and low-scoring outcomes (1-1, 0-0, 1-1, 1-1, 1-1). The historical head-to-head record between these two nations also points to a defensive struggle, with their only two previous encounters ending in a 0-0 draw and a 1-0 victory for Bosnia. Furthermore, the betting market shows significant movement and heavy juice on the Under 2.5 goals at -140, indicating strong market confidence in a tight, low-scoring contest. Without Davies' explosive offensive capabilities, Canada is likely to play a more cautious game, while Bosnia's defensive solidity and strength on set pieces suggest a grind-it-out affair.
